idioms.name: no_any_leakage
idioms.description: Never use 'any' type. Use 'unknown' for truly unknown values and narrow with type guards.
idioms.detection: Detect explicit 'any' annotations. Detect implicit any from missing types. Detect 'as any' casts.
idioms.fix: Replace any with unknown + type guard, or with a proper type/interface. Enable strict and noImplicitAny.

idioms.name: use_discriminated_unions
idioms.description: Use discriminated unions for state machines and variant types instead of optional fields or type assertions.
idioms.detection: Detect interfaces with many optional fields representing different states. Detect type assertions to narrow unions.
idioms.fix: Create a discriminated union with a literal type discriminant field.
idioms.bad_example: interface Result { data?: string; error?: Error; loading?: boolean; }

idioms.name: async_error_handling
idioms.description: Always handle promise rejections. Use try/catch with async/await. Never leave promises unhandled.
idioms.detection: Detect async calls without await or .catch(). Detect missing try/catch around awaited operations.
idioms.fix: Wrap await calls in try/catch. Use .catch() for fire-and-forget promises. Add global unhandledRejection handler.
idioms.bad_example: async function load() { const data = await fetch(url); return data.json(); }

idioms.name: no_as_casts
idioms.description: Avoid 'as' type assertions. They bypass type checking. Use type guards, satisfies, or schema validation instead.
idioms.detection: Detect 'as Type' expressions, especially 'as any' and double assertions.
idioms.fix: Use type guards (is), satisfies operator, or runtime validation (zod, io-ts).

idioms.name: use_strict_mode
idioms.description: Enable strict mode in tsconfig.json. This enables noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, and all strict family checks.
idioms.detection: Detect tsconfig.json without strict: true. Detect strictNullChecks: false.

idioms.name: prefer_readonly
idioms.description: Use readonly for properties that shouldn't change after initialisation. Use Readonly<T> for immutable object types.
idioms.detection: Detect class properties and interface fields that are only assigned in constructor but lack readonly.
idioms.fix: Add readonly modifier. Use Readonly<T> and ReadonlyArray<T> for immutable structures.
idioms.bad_example: class Config { host: string; port: number; constructor(h: string, p: number) { this.host = h; this.port = p; } }
idioms.good_example: class Config { constructor(readonly host: string, readonly port: number) {} }

idioms.name: non_null_assertion
idioms.description: The ! operator bypasses TypeScript's null safety. Use optional chaining (?.) or proper null checks instead.

idioms.name: console_log_in_production
idioms.description: console.log in production code clutters output and can leak sensitive data. Use a proper logger.

idioms.name: enum_antipattern
idioms.description: TypeScript enums have surprising runtime behaviour. Prefer union types or const objects for type safety.
idioms.good_example: type Status = 'active' | 'inactive'; // or: const Status = { Active: 'active', Inactive: 'inactive' } as const;
